params.text:
Communicating with individual needs

Person-centred communication means adapting to the individual — never a one-size-fits-all script.

  • Dementia: keep it calm and simple, one idea at a time, use names not "he/she", and go with their reality rather than correcting.
  • Sight loss: introduce yourself by name, say when you're leaving, describe what's happening.
  • Hearing loss: face the person, don't cover your mouth, use gestures and written notes, check aids.
  • Learning disability: use easy-read, pictures or Makaton, and allow plenty of time.

params.question: It is good practice to correct a person living with dementia every time their account of events differs from reality.

l10n.wrongAnswerMessage: Not quite — repeatedly correcting can cause distress. Meet the person in their reality and reassure them.
l10n.correctAnswerMessage: Correct — avoid arguing with their reality; reassure and redirect instead.
